_comment: Tier-2 Layer-F: validates the catalog AS SHIPPED — the LLM-emit-and-run loop. Per backend, calls live-registry generate_input(backend, physics='poisson', variant='2d'), writes the emitted template to a tempfile, executes it through the backend-correct python (repo .venv for skfem/kratos/ngsolve; ofa-fenicsx conda env for fenics; skip when env missing), asserts exit_code==0 and no Traceback. This is the missing piece between Layer-B (generator doesn't raise) and Layer-C (re-implemented API converges): the actual template the catalog ships actually runs. Covers all 4 Python-introspectable backends (skfem, kratos, ngsolve, fenics). Verified on 2026-06-01: all 4 rc=0, no Traceback. Catches the regression class where a catalog template is well-formed Python (Layer B + Layer E pass) but raises at runtime due to a missing import, wrong PETSc option key, sign error in a manufactured-test source, etc.
